LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT OLIVER YU'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF UNITED FINANCIAL CASUALTY COMPANY'S COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENT OF CCP SEC. 430.41 AND HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT PRIOR RESOLUTION OF THE SMALL CLAIMS ACTION THAT DEFENDANT FILED AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S INSURED MIKHAI NIKITIN HAS PRECLUSIVE EFFECT ON THE CURRENT ACTION. CONTRARY TO DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT, FILING A COUNTERCLAIM IN A SMALL CLAIMS ACTION IS VOLUNTARY RATHER THAN COMPULSORY. (UNIVERSAL CITY NISSAN, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 65 CAL.APP.4TH 203, 205.) PLAINTIFF AND MR. NIKITIN WERE THEREFORE NOT REQUIRED TO FILE A COUNTERCLAIM FOR THE CLAIMS ASSERTED IN THE CURRENT ACTION. THE DOCTRINE OF COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL SHOULD NOT APPLY HERE BECAUSE-IN LIGHT OF THE RULES SET FORTH IN PITZEN V. SUP.CT. (2004) 120 CAL.APP.4TH 1374 AND SANDERS V. WALSH (2013) 219 CAL.APP.4TH 855-IT DOES NOT APPEAR POSSIBLE TO DETERMINE WHETHER A PARTICULAR ISSUE, SUCH AS FAULT FOR THE VEHICLE COLLISION, WAS ACTUALLY LITIGATED AND DECIDED IN THE SMALL CLAIMS COURT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312.JUDGE: CHARLENE P. KIESSELBACH, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: GORDON AIAVAO, CSR #11216, GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG (302/CPK)
ARBITRATOR NANCY LEE HERSH APPOINTED. ARBITRATOR'S JURISDICTION JUN-04-2018 THROUGH SEP-02-2018.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Oliver Yu (415-624-5931) Defendant/Moving party in Pro Per.
No other Appearance

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court admonishes Defendant. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OLIVER YU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant Oliver Yu's demurrer to plaintiff United Financial Casualty Company's complaint is overruled. Defendant failed to comply with the meet and confer requirement of CCP Sec. 430.41 and has failed to show that prior resolution of the small claims action that defendant filed against plaintiff's insured Mikhai Nikitin has preclusive effect on the current action. Contrary to defendant's argument, filing a counterclaim in a small claims action is voluntary rather than compulsory. (Universal City Nissan, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 203, 205.) Plaintiff and Mr. Nikitin were therefore not required to file a counterclaim for the claims asserted in the current action. The doctrine of collateral estoppel should not apply here because-in light of the rules set forth in Pitzen v. Sup.Ct. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1374 and Sanders v. Walsh (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 855-it does not appear possible to determine whether a particular issue, such as fault for the vehicle collision, was actually litigated and decided in the small claims court. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/CPK)
